title: Bridge Essay: Monitoring Attention in Self and Others.
concept: Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.

**Monitoring Attention in Self and Others.** - J Cogn Neurosci (2025). DOI: 10.1162/JOCN.a.51. PMID: 40392116. The ability of the brain to monitor its own attention is important for controlling attention. The ability to reconstruct and monitor the attention of others is important for behavioral prediction and therefore interaction with others. Do the same cortical networks participate in constructing a metacognitive representation of attention, whether one's own or someone else's attention?
